text: Managing a team effectively requires a balance of several skills. First, clear communication is key; you must explain goals and expectations so everyone understands their role. Delegation is also important—assign tasks based on each member’s strengths, rather than doing everything yourself. Regular feedback, both positive and constructive, helps people improve and feel valued. To maintain motivation, recognize achievements and create a supportive environment. Finally, conflict resolution is essential. When disagreements arise, listen to both sides without taking sides and guide the team toward a fair solution. With these practices, a team can stay productive and harmonious.
